clinical_history: Elderly man with extra beats."  What is the rhythm?
diagnosis: This ECG shows underlying sinus bradycardia at about 57 bpm with ventricular bigeminy. On the rhythm strip the first 3 ventricular premature beats (VPBs) have a full compensatory pause but the 4th VPB is interpolated; this VPB is sandwiched between two normal beats without a compensatory pause. The second sinus beat is conducted by the sinus P waves occurring just after the extrasystole.  Nonspecific ST-T changes are noted leads in II, III, aVF and V4-V6.
